posts.content: Our evaluation of OpenAI's GPT-5.5 cyber capabilities. The UK's AI Security Institute previously evaluated Claude Mythos: now they've evaluated GPT-5.5 for finding security vulnerability and found it to be comparable to Mythos, but unlike Mythos it's generally available right now.
posts.highlights: GPT-5.5 is comparable to Claude Mythos in finding security vulnerabilities and is generally available.
posts.worthReading: Highlights the security capabilities of a widely accessible model.

posts.content: Never talk about goblins, gremlins, raccoons, trolls, ogres, pigeons, or other animals or creatures unless it is absolutely and unambiguously relevant to the user's query. — OpenAI Codex
posts.highlights: OpenAI Codex includes an instruction to avoid discussing certain animals unless relevant.
posts.worthReading: Reveals an interesting constraint in AI model instructions.

posts.content: llm 0.31 released: supports GPT-5.5 and adds a verbosity parameter for controlling output detail on OpenAI's latest models.

posts.content: llm 0.32a0 alpha: major backwards-compatible refactor. Models can now be prompted with a list of messages, OpenAI Chat Completions style.

posts.content: Every good story has to end, and after 4 incredible years at @huggingface, it's time for me to start my next adventure. When I joined Hugging Face, we were a small team of 20 and the Hub had less than 5,000 models. Today, We are millions of developers, and thousands of models are released every day and deployed across every major cloud.
posts.highlights: Philipp Schmid announces departure from Hugging Face after 4 years, reflecting on the platform's growth from 20 people and 5,000 models to millions of developers and thousands of daily model releases.
posts.worthReading: Shows a key milestone in the career of a prominent AI/ML figure and the explosive growth of Hugging Face.
